He is the protagonist of Heart of Darkness. He was a seaman, but also a wanderer which led him on his expedition. He is characterized as independent-minded, skeptical and philosophical.
He runs the ivory station in the congo. Due to his greedy personality he falls victim to the indoctrination of his own lack of domestication. Leaving only the darkest parts of his humanity.

Kurtz last words represents what is left of humanity when domestication flies out the window. Not simply quoting his own actions, but that of the darkness that resides within the hearts of every man, woman, and child.
"And this also...has been one of the dark places of the Earth."
The quote represents the good and evil of the world that drives from the actions of the occident. Symbolises the evil of man,mostly european, and their will for imperialism.

Dreams only live within the mind of the dreamer. Our passions and our goals only reside within ourselves. Mentally, we posses a world that the exterior will never see. A world all to ourselves, and all alone.
"Your strength is just an accident arising from the weakness of others."

The quote represents the weakness of a character: that their weakness only finds strength through the feebleness of others. False leadership and false power that a person, or civilization, will abuse.
"It was like a weary pilgrimage amongst hints for nightmares"
